perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall furtherm ore include any and all information regarding the exist ence, nature, subject matter, or progress of or rel ating to the Business Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall not include any and all information regarding the exist ence, nature, subject matter, or progress of or rel ating to the Business Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by explicitly excluding all information about the business purpose from the definition of Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the original intent, as the business purpose's details would naturally be considered confidential. This introduces uncertainty in enforcement as it is unclear whether information regarding the business purpose should be protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Parties’ making available t o each other Confidential Information as defined in Sub- clause 1.1, each Party hereby agrees to the followi ng confidentiality undertakings for and on behalf of itself and for and on behalf of the Party’s employees, adv isors and representatives: • All Confidential Information received by a Party sh all be held in complete confidence by such Party and by the Party’s directors, employees, advisors o r representatives, and shall not without the prior written consent of the other Party be used for any purpose other than in connection with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclos ed or received. • A Party shall not disclose any Confidential Informa tion received by it to any person other than those of Party’s employees, advisors and representatives who are directly concerned with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclosed o r received and who need to know such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes all information about the business purpose from the definition of Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that all Confidential Information received by a Party shall be held in complete confidence, and used only in connection with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclosed or received. Since business purpose is now explicitly NOT considered Confidential Information, this creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: However, Confidential Information shall not include such information which (i) is or has become public ly known other than through a breach of a confidential ity obligation, (ii) has been received from an independent third party without any restrictions or obligation of confidentiality, or (iii) which the receiving Party can demonstrate has been independently develo ped by that Party prior to disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: However, Confidential Information shall not include such information which (i) is or has become public ly known, (ii) has been received from an independent third party, or (iii) which the receiving Party can demonstrate has been independently develo ped by that Party prior to disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the clauses 'other than through a breach of a confidential ity obligation' and 'without any restrictions or obligation of confidentiality' in the exceptions to Confidential Information. Now, even if information becomes publicly known due to a breach of confidentiality, or if a third party provides it with confidentiality restrictions, it is no longer considered confidential. This weakens confidentiality obligations and creates uncertainty about what information remains protected, specifically contradicting the general confidentiality undertakings in section 1.2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Parties’ making available t o each other Confidential Information as defined in Sub- clause 1.1, each Party hereby agrees to the followi ng confidentiality undertakings for and on behalf of itself and for and on behalf of the Party’s employees, adv isors and representatives: • All Confidential Information received by a Party sh all be held in complete confidence by such Party and by the Party’s directors, employees, advisors o r representatives, and shall not without the prior written consent of the other Party be used for any purpose other than in connection with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclos ed or received. • A Party shall not disclose any Confidential Informa tion received by it to any person other than those of Party’s employees, advisors and representatives who are directly concerned with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclosed o r received and who need to know such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the clauses 'other than through a breach of a confidential ity obligation' and 'without any restrictions or obligation of confidentiality'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that all Confidential Information received by a Party shall be held in complete confidence and not used for any purpose other than the intended one. By removing the exceptions related to breaches of confidentiality, the changed text creates a situation where information obtained through a breach would not be considered confidential, directly conflicting with the overall confidentiality undertakings.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information received by a Party sh all be held in complete confidence by such Party and by the Party’s directors, employees, advisors o r representatives, and shall not without the prior written consent of the other Party be used for any purpose other than in connection with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclos ed or received.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information received by a Party sh all be held in confidence by such Party and by the Party’s employees, advisors o r representatives, and shall not without the prior written consent of the other Party be used for any purpose other than in connection with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclos ed or received.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by replacing "complete confidence" with "confidence". This subtly weakens the obligation to maintain confidentiality. While both imply discretion, 'complete confidence' suggests a higher standard of care. This creates uncertainty in determining the level of protection required and contradicts other sections relying on a robust standard of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A Party shall promptly notify the other Party of, w ithout limitation, any unauthorized access, use, co pying or disclosure of Confidential Information of which a Party becomes aware and shall use its best endeav ors to terminate such access, use etc. and shall provid e all reasonable assistance requested by the other Party in this connection.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'complete confidence' to 'confidence' weakens the obligation to maintain confidentiality. The original text set a higher standard. The contradicted text requires 'best endeavors' to prevent unauthorized disclosure. Lowering the standard of confidentiality to just 'confidence' now makes it unclear what level of effort constitutes 'best endeavors' when a lesser degree of confidentiality is now expected, creating a contradiction and uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall ensure (i) that all persons mentio ned in Paragraph 1.2.2 above are made aware, prior to any disclosure, of the confidential nature of th e Confidential Information and of the contents of t his Agreement and (ii) [that such persons are bound by confidentially with respect to the Confidential Information as If they were a party to this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall ensure (i) that all persons mentio ned in Paragraph 1.2.2 above are made aware, prior to any disclosure, of the confidential nature of th e Confidential Information and of the contents of t his Agreement and (ii) that such persons are encouraged to respect the confidential nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by replacing the requirement that employees/advisors be 'bound by confidentially' with a weaker encouragement to 'respect the confidential nature'. This significantly reduces the legal enforceability of confidentiality, contradicting the original intention of holding individuals accountable as if they were parties to the agreement. This introduces ambiguity on the responsibilities and liabilities of those handling confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party shall be liable in accordance with Danis h law for damages (including claims, expenses and indirect losses of whatsoever nature) arising out o f or in connection with that Party’s breach of its obligations under this Agreement, without prejudice to other rights and remedies available including, but not limited to, restrictive injunction and other eq uitable relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires individuals to be 'bound by confidentiality,' while the changed text weakens this to 'encouraged to respect the confidential nature.' The contradicted text states that 'Each Party shall be liable... for that Party’s breach of its obligations under this Agreement.' By weakening the confidentiality requirement, the changed text directly contradicts the section that holds parties liable for breaches, since it introduces ambiguity about whether a breach of the encouraged respect is actually a breach of obligation.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall have in effect and maintain adequ ate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access, disclosure, u se, and/or misappropriation.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall endeavor to have in effect and maintain security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access, disclosure, u se, and/or misappropriation.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the mandatory 'shall have in effect and maintain adequate security measures' to the more discretionary 'shall endeavor to have in effect and maintain security measures'. By replacing 'shall' with 'shall endeavor', it weakens the obligation and makes enforcement more difficult. This contradicts the strict liability clauses outlined in section 3.1, as it becomes unclear if a party is truly liable if they merely 'endeavored' to maintain security.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party shall be liable in accordance with Danis h law for damages (including claims, expenses and indirect losses of whatsoever nature) arising out o f or in connection with that Party’s breach of its obligations under this Agreement, without prejudice to other rights and remedies available including, but not limited to, restrictive injunction and other eq uitable relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the parties 'shall have in effect and maintain adequate security measures'. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'shall endeavor to have in effect and maintain security measures'. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that 'Each Party shall be liable...for damages...arising out o f or in connection with that Party’s breach of its obligations under this Agreement'. The change from 'shall' to 'shall endeavor' makes it unclear whether a party is truly liable if they merely 'endeavored' to maintain security, creating a conflict in obligation and enforcement within the contract, therefore a strong contradiction exists.
